-- Wait, but we don't have injectivity here.
          -- The mistake is in thinking that surjInv hf (f x) = x. This is not always true unless f is injective.
          -- So we cannot use this approach directly.
          -- Better approach: Instead of trying to use surjInv, use the fact that x' is in the image of mk.

          -- But kerLift f x' = f x (by definition).
          -- SoinvFun x' = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (f x)).
          -- But this might not be x unless we have more info.
          -- Alternatively, we might need to use injectivity of kerLift, which we proved earlier.
          -- Actually, no, we can simplify this.
          -- Since x' is Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x, we can compute:
          -- invFun x' = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (kerLift f x")) = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (f x)).
          -- So we need to prove that Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (f x)) = x.
          -- But surjInv hf (f x) could be any preimage of f x.
          -- However, if we take x' = surjInv hf (f x), then f x' = f x.
          -- So x - x' ∈ ker f, hence Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x'.
          -- Thus Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x' = x'.
          -- But by definition of surjInv, f x' = f x.
          -- So perhaps this is not directly useful unless we have injectivity.
          -- Hmm.
          -- Oh, no! Maybe it is easier:
          -- We have that x' = mk (ker f) x.
          -- invFun x' = mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (kerLift f x')) = mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (f x)).
          -- Now, we need to show that invFun x' = x'.
          -- So we need mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (f x)) = mk (ker f) x.
          -- But we have that f (surjInv hf (f x)) = f x, because of surjectivity.
          -- Thus, (surjInv hf (f x)) - x ∈ ker f, hence mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (f x)) = mk (ker f) x.
          -- So we have invFun x' = x'.
          -- Therefore, the composition is indeed x' for any x' in the quotient.
          -- No need for injectivity!
          -- But we need to use the difference being in the kernel.
          have h₇ : f (surjInv hf (f x)) = f x := by
            apply rightInverse_surjInv hf
          have h₈ : surjInv hf (f x) ≡ x [MOD ker f] := by
            -- Here, we need to show that (surjInv hf (f x)) - x ∈ ker f.
            -- But we know that f (surjInv hf (f x)) = f x.
            -- So f (surjInv hf (f x) - x) = 0, hence surjInv hf (f x) - x ∈ ker f.
            have h₉ : f (surjInv hf (f x) - x) = 0 := by
              calc
                f (surjInv hf (f x) - x) = f (surjInv hf (f x)) - f x := by
                  apply map_sub
                _ = f x - f x := by rw [h₇]
                _ = 0 := by simp
            -- Hence, surjInv hf (f x) - x ∈ ker f.
            simpa [Ideal.mem_ker] using h₉
          -- Since they are congruent modulo ker f, they are the same in the quotient.
          simpa [Ideal.Quotient.eq_iff_mem, Ideal.mem_ker] using h₈
        ]

          Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (kerLift f x)) = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (Ideal.toFreqCurrent x + (surjInv hf (kerLift f x) - Ideal.toFreqCurrent x : R)) := by
            ring_nf
            <;> simp_all [Ideal.toFreqCurrent, sub_eq_add_neg]
            <;> aesop
          _ = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (Ideal.toFreqCurrent x) + Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (kerLift f x) - Ideal.toFreqCurrent x : R) := by
            rw [Ideal.Quotient.mk_add]
          _ = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (Ideal.toFreqCurrent x) + 0 := by rw [h₇]
          _ = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (Ideal.toFreqCurrent x) := by simp
      have h₇ : Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (Ideal.toFreqCurrent x) = x := by
        rfl
      calc
        Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf (kerLift f x)) = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (Ideal.toFreqCurrent x) := by rw [h₆]
        _ = x := by rw [h₇]
    }
sorry
